heading: Special use permits
label: 2328a Special use permits

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may issue special permits for uses such as group activities, recreation events, motorized recreation vehicles, and such other specialized recreation uses as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be in the best interest of the Federal Government.

children.label: i
children.text: establish and collect fees associated with the issuance of the permits described in paragraph (1); or

children.label: ii
children.text: accept in-kind services in lieu of those fees.

children.heading: Outdoor recreation equipment
children.label: B Outdoor recreation equipment
children.text: The Secretary may establish and collect fees for the provision of outdoor recreation equipment and services for activities described in paragraph (1) at public recreation areas located at lakes and reservoirs operated by the Corps of Engineers.

children.label: i
children.text: retained at the site collected; and

children.label: ii
children.text: available for use, without further appropriation, solely for administering the special permits under this subsection and carrying out related operation and maintenance activities at the site at which the fees are collected.

children.label: I
children.text: at a lake or reservoir operated by the Corps of Engineers; and

children.label: II
children.text: adjacent to or near a State or local park or recreation area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary determines that cooperative management between the Corps of Engineers and a State or local government agency of a portion of the Corps of Engineers recreation area or State or local park or recreation area will allow for more effective and efficient management of those areas.

children.heading: Restriction
children.label: B Restriction
children.text: The Secretary may not transfer administration responsibilities for any public recreation area operated by the Corps of Engineers.

children.heading: Acquisition of goods and services
children.label: 2 Acquisition of goods and services
children.text: The Secretary may acquire from or provide to a State or local government with which the Secretary has entered into a cooperative agreement under paragraph (1) goods and services to be used by the Secretary and the State or local government in the cooperative management of the areas covered by the agreement.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: The Secretary may enter into 1 or more cooperative management agreements or such other arrangements as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, including leases or licenses, with non-Federal interests to share the costs of operation, maintenance, and management of recreation facilities and natural resources at recreation areas that are jointly managed and funded under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that it is in the public interest for purposes of enhancing recreation opportunities at Corps of Engineers water resources development projects, the Secretary may use funds made available to the Secretary to support activities carried out by State, local, and tribal governments and such other public or private nonprofit entities as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 2 Cooperative agreements
children.text: Any use of funds pursuant to this subsection shall be carried out through the execution of a cooperative agreement, which shall contain such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be necessary in the public interest.
